result:
Policy: refund to the gift card first up to the amount consumed from it, then any remainder to the card. This avoids letting users launder expired-soon gift card value into permanent card credit, and keeps the original payment split intact. If the gift card has already expired by refund time, reissue a new code with the same balance rather than pushing it to the card.

Code path: load the original charge's split (gift_card_amount, card_amount), compute refund_to_gift_card = min(refund_total, gift_card_amount_consumed_remaining), refund_to_card = refund_total - refund_to_gift_card. Credit the gift card row under the same FOR UPDATE lock you use for consumption, then issue the card refund via the PSP. Both writes in one transaction so a PSP failure rolls back the balance credit.
